original_query: select c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum(l_quantity) from customer, orders, lineitem where o_orderkey in ( select l_orderkey from lineitem group by l_orderkey having sum(l_quantity) > 313 ) and c_custkey = o_custkey and o_orderkey = l_orderkey group by c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice order by o_totalprice desc, o_orderdate limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH qualified_orders AS (
    SELECT l_orderkey, SUM(l_quantity) AS sum_qty
    FROM lineitem
    GROUP BY l_orderkey
    HAVING SUM(l_quantity) > 313
)
SELECT c.c_name, c.c_custkey, o.o_orderkey, o.o_orderdate, o.o_totalprice, qo.sum_qty
FROM customer c
JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey
JOIN qualified_orders qo ON o.o_orderkey = qo.l_orderkey
ORDER BY o.o_totalprice DESC, o.o_orderdate
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Converted the correlated subquery with IN to a CTE to avoid scanning lineitem table twice
Eliminated redundant GROUP BY clause since the grouping keys are unique (o_orderkey is a primary key)
Used explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins in the WHERE clause for better readability and performance
Reused the pre-computed sum from the CTE instead of recalculating it in the main query
